method: Static code analysis of dispatchers on sm_75 (CC_TURING) production path. No runtime instrumentation needed — the dispatcher branch structure for NP=2/4/8 is fully determined by compile-time + n_tokens-based static branches.

production_config.arch: qwen35 (dense, NOT moe — production GGUF general.architecture=qwen35)
production_config.weight_dtype: Q4_0 / Q4_0_AR16 (quantized; AutoRound-preserved BF16 for linear_attn projections)

FA_full_attention.rationale: On sm_75 with Q4_0 KV cache, the line-140 condition (!new_mma_available || K->ne[0] != V->ne[0]) is TRUE → wmma_f16 path. Inside wmma_f16, Q->ne[1]<=8 branch (line 81) is hit at all NP∈{2,4,8} → cols_per_block=8.

MMQ_quantized_matmul.rationale: mmq_x_best picker: for ne11<=8, the loop's first iteration with mmq_x=8 gives ntiles_x=1, nparts=1, so loop exits with mmq_x_best=8. Same template at NP=2/4/8.
MMQ_quantized_matmul.note: ggml_cuda_should_use_mmq returns true on sm_75 because int8_mma_available(cc) is TRUE (CC_TURING>=int8_mma threshold) — MMQ path is taken for all NP>=2 quantized matmul. NP=1 may go MMVQ instead (src1->ne[1]==1 && ne[1]<=MMVQ_MAX_BATCH_SIZE).

DeltaNet_recurrence.rationale: Branch `if (n_tokens <= 8)` selects threads_per_block=256 (num_warps=8). All NP∈{2,4,8} hit n_tokens<=8.

headline_finding: The heuristic-dispatch-flip hypothesis is FALSIFIED at NP=2 vs NP=4. All three relevant kernels (FA wmma_f16, MMQ, DeltaNet recurrence) use IDENTICAL template parameters at NP=2 and NP=4 on production sm_75 dense path. The kernel selection is uniform.
implication: Drift is INTERNAL to the kernels, not from heuristic dispatch. Same template, different work amount (M=2 vs M=4 cols of work, or batched recurrence with n_seqs=2 vs n_seqs=4). The kernels' INNER computation is batch-shape sensitive.
candidates_for_s2_3_op_level_capture: MMQ kernel internal: reduction order over K within a tile may depend on M-direction work distribution at small M
FA wmma_f16 kernel internal: padding behavior for Q->ne[1] < cols_per_block=8, or shared-memory K/V loading patterns that depend on actual Q->ne[1]
DeltaNet kernel internal: cross-batch effects via shared memory or scheduling, even though grid blocks should be independent per (batch, head)
next_step: S2.3 (op-level intermediate capture) becomes necessary because the cheap dispatcher trace did NOT find a dispatch flip. Extend cb_eval matching to capture per-op intermediates within each first-divergent layer (Q-il, K-il, V-il, KQV-il, attn_out-il, etc.) to localize WHICH kernel call's output first differs.
